domains.name: Code
domains.description: Source code correctness, style, type safety, generated artifacts, and documentation

domains.name: Data
domains.description: Data hydration and lifecycle (development data; schema mechanics live in code/sync and ops/apply)

domains.name: Infra
domains.description: Local artifact production and project ergonomics (install, build, publish, clean)

domains.name: Operations
domains.description: Environment lifecycle, runtime control, recovery, and operational verification

make.description: Atom satisfied when at least one listed Make target exists in project Makefile(s)

path.description: Atom satisfied when listed paths exist (files or directories) relative to project root

file-content.description: Atom satisfied when file exists and contains required content markers

field.description: Atom satisfied when a JSON/YAML file contains a non-null value at the given path

service.description: Per-package qualifier; values drawn from packages[*].name in forktex.json

env.description: Per-environment qualifier; values drawn from cloud.environments[*].name in forktex.json

atoms.name: Format
atoms.description: Source code conforms to a consistent, auto-enforced style
atoms.evidence: Formatter produces zero diff in --check mode

atoms.name: Lint
atoms.description: Static analysis catches bugs, anti-patterns, and security smells
atoms.evidence: Linter reports zero findings

atoms.name: Typing
atoms.description: Static type system verifies interface correctness across the codebase
atoms.evidence: Type checker reports zero errors

atoms.name: Test
atoms.description: Automated tests verify behaviour at the unit and integration level
atoms.evidence: Test suite passes; coverage report attached

atoms.name: Security
atoms.description: Dependencies and code scanned for known vulnerabilities and security smells
atoms.evidence: Dependency vulnerability scan with zero critical/high findings

atoms.name: License
atoms.description: Source headers and dependency licenses verified against policy
atoms.evidence: License scan output; signed dependency license register

atoms.name: Sync
atoms.description: Derived artifacts (types, migrations, API stubs, RTK queries, docs) kept in sync with their declarative source of truth
atoms.evidence: Re-running sync produces zero diff against committed artifacts

atoms.name: Docs
atoms.description: Project documentation exists and is current — architecture diagrams, API reference, runbooks, ADRs
atoms.evidence: Committed documentation files; auto-generated artefacts current

atoms.name: Manual
atoms.description: System-wide architecture + context manual generated from the project graph (humans + agents). Renders C4 + filesystem inspector + dependency map for humans, plus an AI-consumable bundle of rules / concepts / few-shots for downstream agentic workflows.
atoms.evidence: Generated manual artifact (HTML page + JSON context bundle)

atoms.name: Seed
atoms.description: Development and test data hydration via reproducible packs
atoms.evidence: Seed runner execution log

atoms.name: Install
atoms.description: Project bootstraps to a runnable state on a fresh machine — auto-detects the OS, missing tools (poetry/uv/.venv/node_modules/pyright), and resolves them
atoms.evidence: Lockfile + dependency tree resolved; bootstrap log

atoms.name: Build
atoms.description: Distributable artifacts produced (wheel, sdist, image, bundle, binary)
atoms.evidence: Build artifact (image SHA, wheel hash, bundle digest)

atoms.name: Publish
atoms.description: Built artifacts uploaded to a registry, store, or CDN
atoms.evidence: Registry confirmation; signed publish attestation

atoms.name: Clean
atoms.description: Build artifacts and caches removable from project tree
atoms.evidence: Idempotent cleanup log

atoms.name: Apply
atoms.description: Drive a runtime to its declared state — local process, local compose, or env-orchestrated deploy. Idempotent.
atoms.evidence: Apply log; healthcheck pass; declared-state diff is empty

atoms.name: Destroy
atoms.description: Remove a runtime entirely — terminate processes, tear down env infrastructure
atoms.evidence: Teardown log; absence confirmed

atoms.name: Monitor
atoms.description: Inspect current runtime state — health, metrics, replica status, and live event/log streams
atoms.evidence: Health endpoint response; metric scrape; dashboard state; live log stream

atoms.name: Rollback
atoms.description: Revert a deployed env to the previous known-good version
atoms.evidence: Rollback log; restored version attestation

atoms.name: Acceptance
atoms.description: End-to-end verification that a deployed runtime works against real infrastructure (battle, e2e, load, chaos, pen)
atoms.evidence: End-to-end test report against running env

atoms.name: Backup
atoms.description: Database and volume backups produced and restorable
atoms.evidence: Backup artifact; periodic restore-test log

aliases._description: Profile-level chord names — render as Make targets but don't appear in level/facet/mapping bookkeeping. Each alias resolves to a list of atom invocations.

facets.name: Verification
facets.question: Is the code correct, safe, and consistent?

facets.name: Operational Verification
facets.question: Does the deployed system actually work, and can I recover when it doesn't?

levels.name: Bootstrap
levels.description: New project — manifest exists, nothing else wired

levels.name: Runnable
levels.description: Project installs, runs, and can be torn down

levels.name: Operational
levels.description: Deployable to real environments, with verification and recovery
